brief.headline: Customers are doing the closing work and the arrests photograph fine. The follow-through is the part nobody publishes.
brief.body: Two civilian-intervention stories defined the week. Two armed shoppers cornered Allen Prince in the Pleasant Hill Price Chopper lot on Memorial Day after he killed Amy Coon and wounded a 16-year-old stocker; court documents say he had threatened to shoot his own family eight months earlier and was released on bond with a no-firearms order. In Lynchburg, witnesses chased down a Walmart purse snatcher and held him for police. On the enforcement side, CHP opened a five-day National Organized Retail Crime Blitz with 90 arrests and $153K recovered on day one, while Sonoma County ran a six-agency ORT sweep with LP teams embedded.

callouts.label: Citizen intervention
callouts.text: Three of the week's biggest retail incidents ended with shoppers or bystanders neutralizing the threat. The customer-carry debate is national. The store-level question — what is the front-end script when civilians step in — is still being written one parking lot at a time.

callouts.label: Pre-incident signals
callouts.text: Pleasant Hill is now also a bond-conditions story. A September 2025 family threat call, a shotgun in the closet, a no-firearms order, and eight months later he is in a grocery parking lot with a rifle.

callouts.label: Enforcement vs. conviction
callouts.text: CHP, Sonoma County, Hillsborough Operation D-Fence, and DC Jaws-of-Life ATM indictments all landed in the same window. The number nobody is publishing is what survives intake on the prosecutor's side.

incidents.headline: Pleasant Hill Price Chopper shooting kills customer, wounds 16-year-old employee on Memorial Day
incidents.summary: Allen Prince, 27, opened fire in the parking lot at 4:28 p.m., killing Amy Coon, 45, and wounding teen stocker Ayden. Court documents unsealed May 26 show Cass County deputies were called to the family home in September 2025 after Prince threatened to shoot a relative and admitted to a shotgun in his closet; he was released on bond with a no-firearms order. Formal charges filed May 26 include first-degree murder.

incidents.headline: Potomac CVS pharmacy hit at 3:20 a.m. -- two armed men force four into restroom, clean two safes
incidents.summary: Montgomery County PD says two masked men with handguns forced three employees and one customer into a restroom, took their cellphones, then ordered the pharmacist to open two safes and walked out with prescription medications from the Tuckerman Lane store. Suspects later identified as Jude and Whitaker.

incidents.headline: Mesa Target shooting leaves victim critical, suspect Dylan Stinson booked
incidents.summary: Mesa PD responded to the Target at 1525 S. Power Road just before 8 p.m. Sunday after two men exchanged gunfire inside the store. Dylan Stinson, 21, was booked on aggravated assault and unlawful discharge charges. The victim remains in critical but stable condition and police say the two were not connected.

incidents.headline: Bryan PD uses LPR to grab three Walgreens pharmacy robbery suspects after 3 a.m. hit
incidents.summary: Bryan Police responded to a robbery at the Walgreens on E. 29th Street at 3:11 a.m. Thursday and used license plate reader hits to track the getaway car. Noel McCall, 19, Jayshawn Love, 22, and Andrevious Ramsey, 19, were arrested on foot after a vehicle chase.

incidents.headline: Lexington County deputies still working overnight shooting at Platt Springs Road 7-Eleven
incidents.summary: Lexington County Sheriff deputies responded to a late-night shooting at the 7-Eleven in the 5100 block of Platt Springs Road and found one man with an upper-body gunshot wound. He was transported for treatment. No suspect description has been released and tips are going to Midlands CrimeStoppers.

incidents.headline: Lincoln County woman in custody after firing inside Shell during attempted robbery
incidents.summary: West Virginia State Police say Rebecca Peterson, 42, of Oceana approached the counter at the Shell on Little Coal River Road just before 4 a.m. Friday, pointed a firearm at the clerk, and fired a round into the wall. She was charged with attempted armed robbery and wanton endangerment.

incidents.headline: Three booked at San Antonio Home Depot in printed fake-receipt scheme
incidents.summary: San Antonio police arrested three suspects on organized retail theft and forgery charges after they used a printed fake receipt to walk more than $1,000 in merchandise out of a Home Depot. Officers found the HP printer inside their truck. Rogelio Saldana was booked into the Bexar County jail May 19. Investigators say the receipt-print method is part of a wider crew.

incidents.dollar_display: Store closure, theft 16% of sales at sister store
incidents.headline: Walgreens specialty pharmacy at East 71st Street closes today as Chatham flagship waits for June 4
incidents.summary: The Walgreens specialty pharmacy at 2351 East 71st Street in Chicago pulled the shutters today, with the larger Chatham store at 86th and Cottage Grove set to follow June 4. Walgreens told CBS Chicago theft at the larger store hit 16% of sales, four times the company average. Three South Side aldermen led a Monday protest inside the building.

incidents.headline: Bothell officer fires at car in 7-Eleven lot after passenger points gun
incidents.summary: A Bothell officer stopped a vehicle for an illegal U-turn on Bothell-Everett Highway just after 12:20 a.m. Tuesday and pulled into the 7-Eleven lot. The driver ran on foot. A passenger pointed a firearm at the officer, who fired multiple rounds. Suspects rammed the patrol car and fled; a 19-year-old turned up at a Burien hospital with gunshot wounds.

incidents.headline: Aaron Medina, 26, arrested with machete after $900 Winston-Salem GameStop holdup
incidents.summary: Medina walked into the GameStop on Hanes Mall Boulevard at 6:57 p.m. Monday, drew a machete, took roughly $900 in merchandise, and ran. Winston-Salem PD caught him with the machete and the merchandise. He faces robbery with a dangerous weapon plus concealed-carry and drug counts.

incidents.dollar_display: Over $25,000 in cosmetics across four stores
incidents.headline: Colorado trio set for arraignment in four-store St. Charles County Walgreens makeup haul
incidents.summary: Andrei Constantin, Alin Constantin, and Elisabeta Iancu, all of Colorado, are charged after surveillance caught the trio hitting four St. Charles County, MO Walgreens stores on January 11 and walking out with thousands in cosmetics. Two face accessory stealing charges over $25,000; arraignments scheduled June 17, July 13, and July 21.

incidents.headline: Sacramento serial robber William Ellis sentenced to 251 years for retail parking-lot holdups
incidents.summary: William Ellis, 44, was sentenced Friday in Sacramento County Superior Court for seven counts of robbery and felony evasion across three 2023 retail parking-lot incidents. Two prior strike convictions and multiple aggravating factors stacked the count. Victims handed over wedding rings and family heirlooms at gunpoint while loading vehicles, including families with small children.

incidents.headline: FLOCK LPR places serial pharmacy robber across the street from Odessa CVS
incidents.summary: Wesley Mark Bingham, 68, was arrested May 18 for the May 17 robbery attempt at the CVS on West 42nd Street in Odessa. Officers used the FLOCK system to find his vehicle parked across the street at the time of the attempt. He now faces three counts of aggravated robbery, $165,000 bond, and was out on bond from a May 13 United Supermarkets pharmacy robbery when he hit the CVS.

incidents.headline: Bibb County sheriff hunts masked gunman who hit Macon Family Dollar
incidents.summary: A masked man walked into the Family Dollar at 3190 Mercer University Drive in Macon around 7:30 p.m. Saturday, pulled a handgun on the clerk, and forced her to hand over a register cash box. The suspect fled on foot in a tan shirt, black jeans, and grey-and-white sneakers. Bibb County Sheriff released photos Monday.

incidents.retailer: Apple (gift cards stolen from Sephora, Lululemon, Walgreens)
incidents.dollar_display: $47M Apple shipments to one warehouse, fatal
incidents.headline: NH iPhone-smuggling murder defendant extradited as $47M gift-card ring exposed
incidents.summary: Marco Marquez Vera, 20, of Queens awaits trial in the May 2025 stabbing of warehouse worker Gui Lin, 41, in Derry. NHPR detailed how Chinese organized crime drains stolen gift cards from Sephora and Lululemon, buys iPhones, and ships them through 13 NH warehouses; Apple sent 46,364 devices worth $47M to one Windham facility in 10 weeks.

incidents.headline: 7-Eleven confirms ShinyHunters breach, 600,000 franchisee Salesforce records on the line
incidents.summary: 7-Eleven started mailing notification letters disclosing an April 8 intrusion into the system that holds franchisee documents, SecurityWeek reported May 18. ShinyHunters listed the chain on its leak site April 17 alongside Zara, Carnival, Mytheresa, and Udemy in a broader Salesforce-linked extortion wave. 7-Eleven has only confirmed two Maine residents notified so far.

incidents.headline: Detroit teen takeover outside downtown Gucci leaves 14-year-old shot; 500 teens brawl
incidents.summary: Two groups gathered outside the Gucci store at Library and Farmer Streets in downtown Detroit on Saturday evening when shots were fired. The 14-year-old took a chest wound and survived. DPD detained more than 60 juveniles and recovered the firearm. A 16-year-old and a 17-year-old were charged. Mayor Sheffield is pushing curfew enforcement.

incidents.headline: Arkansas gamer arrested for threatening Walmart mass shooting if hantavirus triggered a U.S. lockdown
incidents.summary: Aaron Keith Bynum, 20, of Oakland, Arkansas, was booked Friday on first-degree terroristic threatening and harassing communications. The FBI National Threat Operations Center got the tip from another player in an online multiplayer game who recorded Bynum saying he would shoot up his local Walmart if the country locked down again. Marion County deputies served the warrant. Bond at $2,500.

incidents.headline: Same Austin gun shop hit twice by the same crew; Fajardo Mondragon, 17, charged after 28-hour spree
incidents.summary: A 15-year-old walked out of Central Texas Gunworks on Saturday with a handgun. His 17-year-old running partner, Cristian Fajardo Mondragon, already had a warrant for stealing a gun from the same store. With a third teen, the crew fired at apartments, two Austin Fire stations, and pedestrians across 12 shootings, wounding four. Mayor Kirk Watson and Chief Lisa Davis say the city needs to reopen the LPR debate.

incidents.headline: Sole Beauty Supply in Clermont hit for the sixth time in a decade, $19K gone
incidents.summary: Two men smashed through the Lake County storefront around 4 a.m. Friday, one using a brick on the front glass, Clermont PD said. Owner Rupert Lespeare had already upgraded cameras and alarms after the prior five hits. Orange County deputies arrested Joshua Leaster, 26, on an unrelated charge and tied him to the burglary. A second suspect is unidentified.

incidents.headline: SFPD ORC task force charges five San Francisco retail theft suspects
incidents.summary: Tyrese Boswell, 24, allegedly hit two Walgreens stores in North Beach and Noe Valley 27 separate times for nearly $40K in cosmetics. He bonded out after a December arrest and ran a week-long Castro Street spree before officers grabbed him again April 4. SFDA ORC vertical prosecutor has the case.

incidents.headline: $1,000 Walmart TV grab in Saraland becomes first-degree robbery and a 90-mph U-Haul chase
incidents.summary: Saraland PD responded to a theft call at Walmart May 14 and learned a suspect had shown a gun, turning it into a robbery. Officers spotted the U-Haul and chased it at speeds up to 90 mph through Chickasaw and Prichard to Mobile, where spike strips and a PIT maneuver ended it. Horace Davis Jr. faces first-degree robbery and pistol possession by a prohibited person. Jamayah Thompson faces second-degree robbery.

incidents.headline: Prince George's PD arrests one-man retail theft ring after 100-plus hits
incidents.summary: Carlos Maurice Wilder, 42, of Riverdale faces charges in more than 100 thefts since January, totaling roughly $50,000. He had nine open theft warrants at the time of arrest. PGPD says Wilder fits the pattern of single repeat offenders driving the bulk of countywide retail loss.

incidents.headline: Las Vegas cracks $418K online fencing operation, recovers 900 stolen items
incidents.summary: LVMPD ORC detectives arrested Roberto Fuentes, 49, after a March investigation traced stolen jewelry, watches, tools, purses, sunglasses, fragrances, cosmetics, and alcohol to his Torrey Pines Drive home. He resold through multiple online platforms. The Nevada Organized Retail Crimes Association inventoried the recovery at over $418,000.

incidents.headline: VBPD indicts three in $1.66M multi-state retail theft ring directed from jail
incidents.summary: Tony McClease ran operations and managed distribution from inside while Alexander Newman boosted product and Chane'l Stith fenced it, prosecutors say. The trio faces organized retail theft and grand larceny conspiracy charges. Losses date to December 2023 across Virginia Beach, Yorktown, Chesapeake, Norfolk, Henrico, and Roxboro, North Carolina.

incidents.headline: Florida AG and HCSO dismantle $12M Home Depot fencing ring in Operation D-Fence
incidents.summary: Fourteen arrests after a six-month undercover run inside Hoover Rengifo's family fencing operation in Lutz. HCSO seized $5M in construction tools and home appliances, $220K cash, and seven vehicles. Home Depot supplied bait product so an undercover detective could sell to Rengifo directly. Crew moved goods across Florida, Tennessee, Kentucky, and Indiana.

incidents.headline: LaGrange clerk pulls firearm on note-passing robber; Thornton, 32, arrested five days later
incidents.summary: A man entered Fuller's Superette on Hamilton Road just after 10 a.m. May 13 and slid the clerk a note demanding the register. She drew a firearm from beneath the counter. He grabbed the note back and walked out. LaGrange PD took Antwanius Thornton, 32, into custody Friday after tips identified him.

incidents.headline: Camden County deputies investigating parking-lot gunfire at Camden Grocery Market
incidents.summary: The Camden County Sheriff's Office responded Wednesday afternoon to reports of two vehicles exchanging gunfire in the parking lot of Camden Grocery Market on State Route 40, west of Kingsland. Both vehicles left before deputies arrived. No victims reported as of Wednesday night and CCSO does not believe there is an active threat.

incidents.headline: Bexar County investigators charge four women in $16K Ulta theft ring
incidents.summary: Estrella Beltran, Roxanne Isaac, and sisters Virginia and Blanca Maldonado were arrested in connection with a nearly year-long run across four San Antonio Ulta locations. Affidavit puts the take above $16,400 in fragrance and skincare. Facial recognition and surveillance identified all four. Each was charged with organized retail theft and bonded out.

incidents.headline: Husband and wife killed inside south Las Vegas Smith's; shoppers tackled the gunman
incidents.summary: Alejandro Alfonso Estrada walked into the Smith's at 9700 S. Maryland Parkway around 11:30 a.m. Tuesday and shot a woman in her mid-40s and her current husband. LVMPD says Estrada had children with the woman and the two were in a custody fight. At least three bystanders subdued him at the scene.

incidents.headline: Visalia man pulls knife on Circle K clerk over Mother's Day beer run
incidents.summary: Gerardo Guzman, 30, walked into the McAuliff Street Circle K just after 3 p.m. Sunday, grabbed two six packs, and threatened to cut the clerk who tried to stop him. He fled on a bicycle and was caught blocks away with a knife, under the influence. Booked on robbery and bicycle DUI.

incidents.headline: Pre-dawn smash-and-grab cleans out South Loop cannabis store, white SUV the getaway
incidents.summary: Three to four suspects broke the front glass at Grasshopper Club on East Roosevelt Road around 3:40 a.m. Monday. One went inside and passed product through the broken window to others who loaded a white SUV. Chicago PD has no one in custody and has not released a dollar figure.

incidents.headline: Ocala's Paddock Mall closes early Mother's Day after firearm brandish
incidents.summary: An altercation inside Paddock Mall Sunday afternoon ended with someone displaying a firearm. No shots were fired, no injuries reported, no arrests as of Sunday night. Mall security cleared the building and shut early on a Mother's Day. Ocala PD released photos of persons of interest.

incidents.headline: Three Baltimore teens arrested after downtown convenience store thefts, gel-ball blaster recovered
incidents.summary: Three boys ages 14, 17, and 18 hit a North Charles Street convenience store repeatedly Sunday around 6 p.m., taking items each trip. On the final pass, the oldest brandished what officers thought was a rifle and knocked over equipment before fleeing. BPD tracked the group four blocks to Light Street and recovered an Orbeez gel-ball blaster.

incidents.headline: Gloucester County deputies arrest Kay Jewelers larceny suspect after multi-county chase
incidents.summary: Deputies got the call to Kay Jewelers at 6738 Fox Center Parkway around 5:15 Saturday afternoon. Gabriele Randazzo, 27, took off as officers arrived. York-Poquoson Sheriff and Virginia State Police joined the pursuit, which ended in York County. Randazzo faces grand larceny, statutory burglary, felony evade and elude, and reckless driving. Held without bond.

incidents.headline: MPD seeks suspect in Save-A-Stop convenience store shooting that left man critical
incidents.summary: Memphis Police released suspect photos Wednesday in connection with a May 8 shooting at the Save-A-Stop 1 at Pendleton and Deadrick in Orange Mound. The suspect followed the victim inside, drew a handgun, shot him, and fled south on Pendleton. Victim remains in critical condition.

incidents.headline: Convicted felon arrested in five-store rifle robbery spree across the Lowcountry
incidents.summary: Marquell Lovon Holmes, 37, was booked Monday after a May 2 rifle robbery at a University Boulevard Family Dollar in North Charleston. Officers searching his car found a loaded AR-15 with a 30-round magazine. Holmes is also charged in the April 15 Hollywood Family Dollar holdup and the April 25 Johns Island Dollar General job.

incidents.headline: Cary, NC trading card shop hit for $32,826 in pre-dawn smash-and-grab; two in custody by morning
incidents.summary: James Mitchell Fillingame, 20, and a 17-year-old juvenile broke a front window at Triangle Area Trading Cards around 5:20 a.m. Wednesday using a shopping cart and a tool, then loaded duffel bags with Pokemon and Magic cards. Cary PD recovered everything. Fillingame is held on $15,000 bail.

incidents.headline: Knifepoint heist at Orioles Team Store: 49-year-old grabbed two jerseys, then drew a blade
incidents.summary: Baltimore Police arrested a 49-year-old Wednesday morning after a 10 a.m. armed robbery at the Orioles Team Store on West Camden Street. He pulled two jerseys off a wall, was confronted by an employee, then drew a knife and ran. Officers caught him on N. Eutaw Street.

incidents.headline: Arizona cargo crew hits second BNSF train; investigators trace ring back to southern California
incidents.summary: Yavapai County deputies say a crew transferred four pallets of tech off a semi at an Ash Fork truck stop in 11 minutes April 14, then hit a railcar northwest of Williams. Eleven defendants were indicted in a separate $202K Nike haul on the same line. Investigators told AZ Family Wednesday they believe the operation runs out of southern California.

incidents.headline: Two killed, three wounded in Carrollton Korean grocery shooting; suspect caught at H Mart
incidents.summary: Sung Ho Han, 69, opened fire on business associates at Gwangjang Market in K Towne Plaza on Tuesday morning, then killed a man at an apartment four miles south. Carrollton officers grabbed him on foot at the Old Denton Road H Mart by noon and he confessed to a financial dispute.

incidents.headline: Shreveport grocery employee charged with attempted murder after shooting customer
incidents.summary: Malik Ahmad Omar, 21, an employee at Consumers Grocery on Hollywood Avenue, was arrested after surveillance confirmed he shot a customer inside the store during a dispute. The victim was found across the street with a life-threatening abdominal gunshot wound.

incidents.headline: Federal grand jury charges four Californians in multi-state pharmacy burglary conspiracy
incidents.summary: The Southern District of Illinois announced charges against Anthony Ellison, 25, of Moreno Valley, Idani Johnson, 34, of Compton, and two co-defendants for conspiring to burglarize pharmacies and distribute controlled substances. The crew is accused of traveling from California to Ohio, then hitting drugstores in Missouri, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Tennessee, Pennsylvania, and Idaho between April 2024 and February 2026.

incidents.headline: Hammer-wielding crew cleans out Zales at Pleasanton's Stoneridge mall in broad daylight
incidents.summary: A crew with hammers and pepper spray smashed two cases at Zales inside Stoneridge Shopping Center around 11:30 a.m. Monday and emptied them. Workers and shoppers ran. Pleasanton PD has not said how many were in the crew, what was taken, or whether anyone is in custody.

incidents.headline: $600K Home Depot fencing ring busted at SoCal swap meet
incidents.summary: California Highway Patrol Southern Division ORC Task Force served warrants April 28 on two suspects and recovered $600,000 in stolen Home Depot tools. The pair bought from boosters and resold through a Los Angeles County swap meet that CHP also shut down.

incidents.headline: Walgreens shutters Chatham store at 86th and Cottage Grove, blames theft and violence
incidents.summary: Walgreens told CBS Chicago the Chatham location ran significantly higher theft and violent incidents than peers, and it closes June 4. Alderman William Hall (6th Ward) is pushing for modifications instead of closure.

incidents.headline: Suspects walked merchandise out of Waipahu Town Center store, then assaulted security
incidents.summary: A man and a woman selected merchandise inside a Waipahu Town Center store at 10:56 a.m. Sunday and walked out without paying. When a security guard confronted them in the parking lot, the male suspect assaulted him and the pair fled east on foot. Honolulu PD released suspect images Tuesday.

incidents.headline: Everest ransomware crew claims Fiserv, the payment plumbing for US retailers
incidents.summary: Ransomware.live confirmed Fiserv on the Everest leak site May 3. Fiserv runs payment processing, merchant acquiring, and core banking software used by retailers and banks across the country, with no public timeline yet on data exfiltration or ransom demand.

incidents.headline: CHP-led National Organized Retail Crime Blitz opens with 90-plus arrests and $153K recovered
incidents.summary: California Highway Patrol's Organized Retail Crime Task Force teamed with 100-plus law enforcement agencies and major retailers for a coordinated five-day national push running May 26 through May 30. Day-one operations focused on shopping centers, malls, and high-traffic retail corridors statewide, with multiple suspects tied to crews operating beyond California state lines.

incidents.headline: Six-agency Sonoma County ORT sweep books arrests across five cities with LP teams embedded
incidents.summary: Rohnert Park Department of Public Safety, Sonoma County Sheriff, Windsor PD, Santa Rosa PD, Petaluma PD, and the Sonoma County DA Investigators ran a single-day Organized Retail Theft enforcement operation May 20 alongside retail loss prevention officers. Santa Rosa PD confirmed two arrests with charges pending, and at least one suspect is being held without bail.

incidents.headline: Bystanders restrain Lynchburg Walmart purse snatcher until LPD arrives
incidents.summary: Anthony Alexander Heath Hamilton, 21, grabbed a woman by the arms and stole her purse in the Walmart parking lot at 3227 Old Forest Road just before 11:23 a.m. Sunday. Witnesses chased him down on foot and held him until officers arrived. Property recovered, Hamilton booked into Blue Ridge Regional Jail without bond on robbery, grand larceny, and credit-card theft counts.
